agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on new Sign Bylaw replacement
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is considering a replacement of the 1972 Sign Bylaw. Key changes include: new sign types (blade, digital, projected, sky signs, sign bands); digital sign brightness regulations; sky signs allowed only on buildings over 22,000 m² where the business occupies 25%+ of floor area; illumination restricted in lanes fronting residences (no illumination 11pm–7am); signs must not inhibit street tree growth (4.0 m clearance); city-owned billboards over 19.0 m² permitted; and new penalty provisions for traffic/structural hazards, tree/wire interference, and obstructing an inspector. This is a proposal only — not yet enacted.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for council decision and any public hearing dates — new rules will affect signage size, type, illumination, and permit requirements for Burnaby businesses.

agenda_briefs.article_title: Committee of the Whole Agenda — May 25, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to consider housing delivery policy options
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to discuss policy options to support housing delivery during tough economic times. A verbal report will outline possible changes to rules or incentives to help get new housing built. This is a proposal only — no decisions have been made yet. The goal is to keep housing projects moving forward despite high costs and market challenges.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — proposed policy changes could affect your development or construction projects.

3.2
VERBAL REPORT: POLICY CONSIDERATIONS FOR HOUSING DELIVERY IN CURRENT MARKET CONDITIONS
Purpose: To discuss policy options to support housing delivery during challenging economic times.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Committee of the Whole Agenda — May 25, 2026

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved new Sign Bylaw and enforcement changes for Burnaby
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has voted to prepare a new Sign Bylaw and amend the enforcement bylaw. Staff will also create a guide for applicants that explains the application process, requirements, and what a completed sign permit looks like. This decision is final — the new rules will replace the current sign regulations once adopted.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Watch for the new Sign Bylaw and applicant guide — review how the updated rules may affect your business signage before they take effect.
minutes_briefs.source_body: In conclusion, the speakers advised that next steps would be a report to Council for a new sign bylaw and staff will prepare a guide for applicants describing the application process, requirements and completed sign permit.
minutes_briefs.article_title: Committee of the Whole Minutes — May 25, 2026

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved policy amendment report for housing relief measures
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved staff to bring forward a policy amendment report for housing relief measures. This means staff will now notify applicants and industry partners, update policy documents, and work with applicants who choose to opt in to prepare legal agreements. A Housing Agreement Bylaw will be brought forward to Council at a later meeting. This decision is final and moves the housing relief process forward.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you are a housing applicant in Burnaby, contact the city to learn how opting in to the new housing relief measures may affect your project.
minutes_briefs.source_body: In conclusion, L. Garnett advised that the next steps include bringing a policy amendment report forward to Council. Subject to Council approval, staff will notify applicants and industry partners, update policy and implementation documents, and work with applicants who choose to opt in to prepare legal agreements. A Housing Agreement Bylaw will subsequently be brought forward to Council.
minutes_briefs.article_title: Committee of the Whole Minutes — May 25, 2026
